genome_divergence: SNP divergence is het-weighted per Peter 2018's published convention; length-changing indel alleles are scored by exact edit distance.
pangenome_and_ko_burden: Core = present in all 1,011 isolates (Peter 2018's own definition; we recover 4,942 vs their published 4,940). NOT comparable to a KO one-for-one: a KanMX deletion is a verified complete null in an isogenic background; a natural variant allele is unverified, selected-upon and compensated.
regional_nucleotide_diversity: The species-aware transformer's input (CDS + 1,000 bp upstream + 297 bp downstream) covers 94.7% of the genome and captures 93.3% of all nucleotide diversity. π from Peter 2018's 1011Matrix.gvcf (1,753,947 variants); regions assigned by precedence CDS > upstream > downstream > intergenic.
de_comparison_ko_vs_natural: Identical rule on both arms: |log2 FC| > log2(1.7) AND BH-adjusted p < 0.05 (Kemmeren 2014's own criterion). Caudal has no published p-values, so its noise model is estimated from its 29 replicate cultures and applied the same way — without that control its count would be 1,011, not 160.
genotype_phenotype_coupling: Each point is one of the 943 natural isolates. If genome bits translated directly into transcriptome bits, these would be tight lines.
codon_usage_deviation: Per-isolate codon frequencies over intronless reference ORFs, minus S288C's. A 1‰ shift is one codon in a thousand.
bit_ledger: L_C = gzip codelength (zlib level 6, streamed) — a computable UPPER BOUND on Kolmogorov complexity, not an entropy, and a loose one (gzip leaves 1.4–5× vs a large-window compressor). The right-hand ratio is arithmetic on those two codelengths; any reading of it as 'what a model must infer' is interpretation, not measurement.
